pages.raw_text:
A. NJ. Macho, the employment
agent was set free yesterday after
a hearing on charge of defrauding
Nick, Slovenian. out of ST. There
was no evidence. About 2 weeks
ago Macho's case was first called.
Then there were Is other foreigners
lined up to testify against the en-
ployment man. But the prosecut-
ing attorneys Office was not repre-
sented. Macho had no counsel at
the time. He asked for a postpone
ment for a few days. Justice Hin
kle granted his request. On the
date set Macho appeared and the
prosecuting attorney asked for a
further postponement not because
he did not have his evidence "in
shape 1t certainly was in queer
shape yesterday for none of the wit
nesses appeared and it was a case
of one mans word against another.
This has been the usual fate of
employment agency caSes-trial
postponed, witnesses all gone when
case is called.
